---
title: "Equivalence with tna: variable session lengths"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>",
  message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10, fig.height = 7, dpi = 150
)
source("_equivalence-helpers.R")
```

12 sessions with deliberately bimodal lengths: 8 short sessions of
length 4 and 4 long sessions of length 80. The resulting wide session
matrix is mostly `NA` -- every short session contributes only 4
non-`NA` cells but is padded out to 80 columns. The engines have to
agree on how to ignore the padding when computing transitions; this
article confirms they do.

The pipeline below runs the same htna ↔ tna ↔ Nestimate three-way
equivalence checks as the
[main equivalence article](tna-equivalence.html).

## Setup

```{r}
library(tna)
library(htna)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

ds <- ds_variable_lengths()
```

```{r child = "_equivalence-body.Rmd"}
```
